description: 12-aa affinity/epitope tag (PDRVRAVSHWSS) recognized by BC2 nanobody with sub-nanomolar affinity; enables immunodetection, pull-down, and dSTORM super-resolution labeling

references.authors: Braun MB, Traenkle B, Koch PA, Emele F, Weiss F, Poetz O, Stehle T, Rothbauer U
references.title: Peptides in headlock – a novel high-affinity and versatile peptide-binding nanobody for proteomics and microscopy

references.authors: Virant D, Traenkle B, Maier J, Kaiser PD, Bodenhöfer M, Schmees C, Vojnovic I, Pisak-Lukáts B, Endesfelder U, Rothbauer U
references.title: A peptide tag-specific nanobody enables high-quality labeling for dSTORM imaging

references.authors: Mentzelopoulou A, Liu C, Moschou PN
references.title: Protein Detection and Localization in Plant Cells Using Spot-Tagging
Epitope tag and method for detection and/or purification of tagged polypeptides

label: Spot_tag
note: 12-aa affinity/epitope tag (PDRVRAVSHWSS); affinity-optimized variant of the BC2-tag (PDRKAAVSHWQQ, residues 16-27 of human β-catenin P35222). Four substitutions relative to BC2T: K4V, A5R, Q11S, Q12S. Sequence first publicly disclosed as SEQ ID NO:33 (pTag1) in patent US20170137467A1.

provenance.sequence_source: US20170137467A1 (patent: "Epitope tag and method for detection and/or purification of tagged polypeptides", Rothbauer et al., ChromoTek GmbH; filed 2016-11-16, published 2017-05-18), SEQ ID NO:33 (pTag1) = PDRVRAVSHWSS, 100% identity over 12 aa; independently fetched from https://patents.google.com/patent/US20170137467A1/en and sequence confirmed. Parent epitope BC2T (PDRKAAVSHWQQ) is residues 16-27 of human β-catenin (UniProt P35222), originally described in Braun et al. 2016 (PMID 26791954, doi:10.1038/srep19211).

functional_claims.label: Acts as an affinity/epitope tag enabling pull-down and immunodetection via cognate BC2 nanobody

value.binding_mechanism: nanobody headlock — CDR3 Arg106 forms a salt bridge with Asp1 and packs against Val4/Arg5; all four substituted positions relative to BC2T contribute to affinity enhancement

source.quote: BC2 nanobody binds the BC2-tag peptide with KD ~1.4 nM via a headlock mechanism

functional_claims.label: KD ~0.7 nM for Spot-tag (pTag1, PDRVRAVSHWSS) with BC2 nanobody — approximately 2-fold improved over parent BC2T

value.method: surface plasmon resonance or equivalent; reported in patent US20170137467A1

functional_claims.label: Functional in mammalian cell lines (vimentin, tubulin, actin, lamin, LC3B, membrane proteins labeled in dSTORM)

value.context: fusion protein N- or C-terminal tag

source.quote: labeling of vimentin, tubulin, actin, lamin, LC3B, and membrane proteins in mammalian and yeast cells

functional_claims.label: Functional in plant cells (Arabidopsis thaliana); demonstrated with KIN7.3 microtubule motor

source.quote: Spot-tag application in plant cells (Arabidopsis); proof-of-concept with KIN7.3 microtubule motor

functional_claims.label: Enables dSTORM super-resolution microscopy labeling via Spot-tag/BC2-nanobody system

value.format: bivalent nanobody; site-specifically labeled with organic fluorophores
value.advantage: small tag size (12 aa) minimizes steric perturbation; high affinity enables efficient target capture

source.quote: first peptide-tag-specific nanobody used in dSTORM; bivalent nanobody format

functional_claims.label: Spot-tag (PDRVRAVSHWSS) is an affinity-optimized variant of BC2-tag (PDRKAAVSHWQQ); differs at positions 4 (K→V), 5 (A→R), 11 (Q→S), 12 (Q→S)

value.effect: ~2-fold affinity improvement over BC2T (KD 0.7 nM vs ~1.4 nM)

source.quote: BC2-tag peptide (PDRKAAVSHWQQ) is residues 16-27 of human beta-catenin
